title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins, who resigned as U.S. Attorney in 2023, is running for Suffolk County District Attorney again.
Current DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion are also candidates in the upcoming DA election.
Rollins resigned amid allegations of meddling in DA races and was suspended from practicing law in Massachusetts.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford firefighter Nick Spinale, 28, critically injured after falling 40 feet during training at Massachusetts Firefighting Academy.
Spinale suffered severe injuries, requiring multiple surgeries and intensive care at UMass Memorial Medical Center in Worcester.
Chelmsford Firefighters Local 1839 seeks community support and highlights the risks firefighters face in their training and duties.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox's new second base celebration is inspired by a TikTok video from outfielder Roman Anthony.
The TikTok video features Roman Anthony dancing to Mariah Carey's 'Obsessed' during his high school days.
Roman Anthony, 21, has a .217 batting average in 2026, with three extra-base hits and two RBIs in 12 games.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Adams police chief Kevin Scott Kelley charged with fraud related to $19,123.15 in ineligible overtime claims.
Kelley allegedly submitted false reimbursement claims under a traffic enforcement grant from MassDOT between 2024 and 2025.
Kelley was indicted and will be arraigned on May 4 in Berkshire Superior Court after a State Police investigation.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions demand Chief Charles Gray's removal following Kelsey Fitzsimmons's acquittal and leadership concerns.
Unions cite lack of body cameras and low morale as reasons for no confidence vote against Chief Gray.
Acquitted officer Fitzsimmons plans a civil lawsuit against town officials post-acquittal.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics are reportedly interested in trading for Milwaukee Bucks star Giannis Antetokounmpo.
Antetokounmpo is under contract through 2028, but Milwaukee might explore trade options due to team struggles.
Acquiring Antetokounmpo could push the Celtics deep into the luxury tax with existing Supermax contracts.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox achieved their first series win of the season, defeating the Brewers 5-0.
Pitcher Sonny Gray improved his performance with 6 1/3 scoreless innings, lowering his ERA to 2.76.
Tyler Samaniego debuted in the major leagues, striking out the side in a scoreless eighth inning.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu unveiled a $4.9 billion budget for FY 2027, proposing a 2.1% spending increase.
Factors like police overtime, snow removal, and rising health care costs contributed to a $48.4 million budget deficit.
A $1.7 billion Boston School Committee budget for FY 2027 was approved, reflecting a 5.4% increase and including layoffs.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are the finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame in 2026.
Gronkowski, a first-time nominee, retired as a Patriot and has the most touchdowns in team history.
Vinatieri, inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame, holds NFL records for points scored and field goals made.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins signed top prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract starting this 2025-26 season.
Hagens, 19, has played six games for Providence Bruins, scoring one goal and three assists.
Hagens's signing makes him ineligible for AHL playoffs; Bruins may prioritize his NHL playoff appearances.
